channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Miguel Amaya has 2 doubles and 5 RBIs to lead the Cubs past the Diamondbacks 10-6
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — Miguel Amaya hit two doubles and had five RBIs, Ian Happ added a homer, double and three RBIs and the Chicago Cubs beat the Arizona Diamondbacks 10-6 on Thursday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Texas Tech rallies from 16 points down to beat Arkansas 85-83 in first OT game in March Madness
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Darrion Williams could barely make a shot for the first 30 minutes. He didn't miss when it mattered most, sending Texas Tech to an improbable spot in the Elite Eight.
item.dc:creator: JOSH DUBOW, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Flagg flies in March Madness and Duke wins 100-93 over Arizona to reach the Elite Eight
item.description: Duke stud Cooper Flagg put on a skills clinic and overcame an onslaught from Arizona and Caleb Love, finishing with 30 points, six rebounds and seven assists in a 100-93 NCAA Tournament victory that pulled the Blue Devils within one win of the Final Four.
item.dc:creator: Eddie Pells, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Ovechkin leads entire Capitals team in tribute to Wild's Fleury with special postgame handshake line
item.description: ST. PAUL, Minn. (AP) — Alex Ovechkin didn't score for Washington, staying at 889 career goals and six away from breaking Wayne Gretzky's all-time record. Ovechkin did initiate a memorable moment on the ice in Minnesota on Thursday night, though.
item.dc:creator: DAVE CAMPBELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Flyers fire coach John Tortorella in midst of another losing season
item.description: John Tortorella popped off after another Philadelphia loss — the sixth straight on his watch — and declared he was not interested in learning how to coach a losing team in another empty season. It's not his worry anymore.
item.dc:creator: DAN GELSTON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jazz lose for 14th time in last 15 games, 121-110
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (AP) - Alperen Sengun had 33 points and 10 rebounds, Amen Thompson finished with 16 points, 10 rebounds and five assists, and the Houston Rockets beat the Utah Jazz 121-110 on Thursday night. The Jazz have now lost 14 of its last 15 games.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Alabama makes March Madness record 25 3s and romps past BYU 113-88 to reach Elite Eight
item.description: Alabama broke the 35-year-old March Madness record for 3-pointers with 25 and Mark Sears scored 30 of his 34 points on 3s in an 113-88 victory over BYU in an NCAA Tournament East Region semifinal.
item.dc:creator: Dan Gelston, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Padres rally in seventh inning to win home opener against Braves
item.description: The Padres kicked off the 2025-2026 season with a comeback victory over the Atlanta Braves Thursday at Petco Park.
item.dc:creator: Domenick Candelieri, Fox 5

item.title: Flyers fire head coach John Tortorella just before season’s end after questionable comments
item.description: The Philadelphia Flyers announced the decision to part ways with head coach John Tortorella Thursday, just days after he made questionable remarks about his interest in coaching.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: White Sox hammer Angels in lopsided Opening Day win
item.description: CHICAGO (WGN) — The weather on White Sox Opening Day mirrored their franchise outlook heading into 2025. Murky, gray and dreary with little to be excited about besides the fact baseball season is all the way back. Good thing the White Sox played well in spite of the melancholy conditions.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Reigning champ Ilia Malinin of US leads Japan's Yuma Kagiyama at figure skating world championships
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — American figure skating sensation Ilia Malinin sheepishly grinned when his closest rival, Japanese star Yuma Kagiyama, said what seems to be on everyone's minds these days: “I'm starting to think he's invincible.
item.dc:creator: DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NBA and FIBA to take next steps toward adding a new European league, sides announce
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The NBA may soon be significantly expanding its presence in European basketball, in the form of partnering with FIBA on a new league that the sides have been talking about for many years.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New San Diego Padres mural in North Park nears completion
item.description: A new San Diego Padres-themed mural in North Park replacing a previous work of public art that had been defaced earlier this year by fans of the rival Los Angeles Dodgers is nearing completion, just in time for opening weekend.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, Fox 5

item.title: SF's Chase Center hosting NCAA Men's Basketball games Thursday, Saturday
item.description: (BCN) -- San Francisco is hosting two games at Chase Center on Thursday in the NCAA Men's Basketball Championship tournament. At 4:39 p.m., University of Florida, which is the No. 1 seed in the West region of the tournament's bracket, plays No. 4 University of Maryland.
item.dc:creator: Bay City News, KRON4

item.title: World Series champion has 1 major worry for the Mets going into the season
item.description: World Series champion AJ Pierzynski expressed concern over the New York Mets starting pitching going into the season during an appearance on OutKick's "Don't @ Me with Dan Dakich.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Royal Riot Podcast Exclusive: Ariath Piol talks goals, red cards, and his journey to RSL
item.description: Royal Riot Podcast hosts Hayden and Mattey sit down with Ariath Piol in an exclusive interview where the player opens up about his journey to RSL, the emotions, lessons, and support he has received from the club and more.
item.dc:creator: Royal Riot Podcast, ABC 4

item.title: Georgia receiver Nitro Tuggle reached speeds of over 100 mph before reckless driving arrest: report
item.description: The latest Georgia football player to be arrested on driving-related crimes was said to be traveling speeds of over 100 mph before his arrest, according to a report.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NCAA Tournament regionals: A look at Chicagoland connections, program history and more for the men's basketball Sweet 16
item.description: The 2025 NCAA Men's Basketball Tournament resumes Thursday night, and it's down to the Sweet 16. It'll be the Elite Eight after the regional semifinals on Thursday and Friday and then the Final Four after the regional finals on Saturday and Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: Warriors' Stephen Curry won't play in Miami, 2nd straight game missed with pelvic contusion
item.description: MIAMI (AP) — Stephen Curry is going to take a little more time to heal. Curry remained out of Golden State's lineup Tuesday night for a game against the Miami Heat, the second straight game he has missed with a pelvic contusion.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Stanford football coach Troy Taylor fired after allegations
item.description: (KRON) -- Stanford football head coach Troy Taylor has been fired, team officials announced Tuesday, following a report that he was investigated twice for allegedly mistreating staffers.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mense, KRON4

item.title: How the San Diego Padres got their name
item.description: The San Diego Padres' name is a tribute to the city's history, with the team's name coming from the Franciscan Friars who founded the area.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, Fox 5

item.title: USC star JuJu Watkins suffers season-ending knee injury in March Madness win over Mississippi State
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — JuJu Watkins was carried off the floor with a season-ending knee injury in the first quarter of top-seeded Southern California’s game against Mississippi State in the NCAA Tournament on Monday night.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Stephen Curry listed as questionable for Golden State's Tuesday game in Miami
item.description: Stephen Curry is back with the Golden State Warriors after missing the first game of the team's road trip with a pelvic contusion, and could play when his team takes on the Miami Heat in Jimmy Butler's return game Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Bears sign DB Nick McCloud to 1-year contract
item.description: The Chicago Bears have signed defensive back Nick McCloud to a one-year contract, bringing his NFL experience from the Bengals, Giants, and 49ers to the team.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: WWE legend Kane says Dems have 'real issue' with masculinity
item.description: WWE Hall of Famer Kane — also known as Glenn Jacobs, mayor of Knox County, Tennessee — joined "Morning in America.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: BYU fans celebrate Sweet 16 bid with Cougars
item.description: PROVO, Utah (ABC4 Sports) - Hours after BYU pulled out a 91-89 thriller over Wisconsin to advance to its first Sweet 16 since 2011, hundred of diehard BYU fans showed up at Provo Airport at 1:30 in the morning to welcome the conquering heroes back from Denver. "What a game!" said one BYU fan.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Brackets busted: No perfect March Madness brackets remain after Sunday's games
item.description: Brackets, busted. The handful of remaining perfect brackets in the NCAA Tournament busted out on Sunday, ending the hopes of millions against exceptionally long odds. The final perfect brackets on Yahoo Sports and CBS Sports were shredded with Saturday's games.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: In March Madness, BYU gets a stop, then gets to go ... to Sweet 16 with 91-89 win over Wisconsin
item.description: The BYU Cougars led the entire game Saturday but still had to make one, final defensive stop against Wisconsin's irrepressible guard, John Tonje, to seal a 91-89 victory over the Badgers and the program's first trip to the Sweet 16 since 2011 — the days of Jimmer Fredette.
item.dc:creator: EDDIE PELLS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump makes NCAA men's wrestling championships his latest sports-focused trip
item.description: BRIDGEWATER, N.J. (AP) — President Donald Trump attended the NCAA wrestling championships on Saturday night for the second time in three years, the latest example of how he has mostly limited travel early in his new term to trips built around sports events.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Sweet 16: Calipari guides Arkansas past Pitino, St. John's 75-66 for 16th trip to regional semifinal
item.description: John Calipari is heading to the Sweet 16 for the 16th time, and this one could be the sweetest of all.
item.dc:creator: JIMMY GOLEN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: No. 12 South Florida Falls To No. 5 Tennessee, 101-66, In The First Round Of NCAA Tournament
item.description: No. 12 seed South Florida (23-11) fell to No. 5 seed Tennessee (23-9), 101-66, in the first round of the NCAA Tournament at Value City Arena on Friday night.
item.dc:creator: USF Athletics, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Blackhawks face Blues in St. Louis
item.description: Chicago Blackhawks (20-40-9, in the Central Division) vs. St. Louis Blues (35-28-7, in the Central Division) St. Louis; Saturday, 3 p.m. EDT BOTTOM LINE: The St. Louis Blues host the Chicago Blackhawks with a four winning streak intact. St.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Boxing great George Foreman passes away
item.description: (KRON) -- Boxing legend, heavyweight champion and grilling entrepreneur George Foreman has passed away, his family announced in an Instagram post on Friday. Our hearts are broken. With profound sorrow, we announce the passing of our beloved George Edward Foreman Sr.
item.dc:creator: Tor Smith, KRON4
